message: Node started: Verify the existence of k(n)=2. A multiple of 2023 has two ones in binary if 2^a + 2^b is divisible by 2023. This is equivalent to 2^(a-b) = -1 mod 2023 (assuming a > b). Test this by checking if -1 is in the cyclic subgroup generated by 2 modulo 2023. Use the Chinese Remainder Theorem on the prime factors of 2023 (7, 17, and 289). Specifically, check if there exists an x such that 2^x = -1 mod 7, 2^x = -1 mod 17, and 2^x = -1 mod 289. If such an x exists, then k(n)=2 is possible. If not, check if k(n)=3 is possible by testing if 2^a + 2^b + 2^c is a multiple of 2023.
